centroid.description: Will calculate the centerpoint for all polygons and linestring and *add* them to geometries
centroid.name: centroid

checked.description: A list of IDs of selected entities for the table.
checked.name: checked

cidoc_classes.description: Retrieve entities based on the specified CIDOC classes.
cidoc_classes.name: cidoc_classes

column.description: Choose one column to sort the results by. Default value is name.
column.name: column

count.description: Show integer count of how many entities would the result give back
count.name: count

depth.description: Specifies the number of connection hops to include in the query. Higher values may significantly increase processing time. Default is 1.
depth.name: depth

entities.description: The entity IDs to be requested.
entities.name: entities

exclude_system_classes.description: Excludes entities with the selected system classes from the network.
exclude_system_classes.name: exclude_system_classes

export.description: Export the entities in either a simple CSV format or a ZIP file containing CSV files optimized for network analysis.
export.name: export

fileId.description: Specific ID of a file entity.
fileId.name: fileId

first.description: Starts the result set at the specified entity ID.
first.name: first

format.description: Specify the format for the returned results.
format.name: format

geometry.description: Filters the geometries to be included in the response. The default is 'gisAll'.
geometry.name: geometry

image_size.description: Select the desired image size to display. The available values are fixed but can be customized for each OpenAtlas instance. The 'thumbnail' size is 200px, and the 'table' size is 100px.
image_size.name: image_size

last.description: Starts the result set after the specified entity ID.
last.name: last

limit.description: Limits the number of entities returned in the response. A lower value may improve performance. The default is 20. Set to 0 to return all available entities.
limit.name: limit

linked_entities.description: The entity IDs for which all linked entities will be retrieved.
linked_entities.name: linked_entities

linked_to_ids.description: Displays only entities that are linked to the specified IDs.
linked_to_ids.name: linked_to_ids

locale.description: Choose language for system inherent labels
locale.name: locale

map_overlay.description: Will add the map overlay information for images if available.
map_overlay.name: map_overlay

page.description: Specifies the page number to retrieve in a paginated result set.
page.name: page

place_hierarchy.description: Will include the whole place hierarchy. This means that instead of only getting entities linked to the requested entity, all entities, which are connected with the property *P46* are included in the result set. This can significantly increase the load time.
place_hierarchy.name: place_hierarchy

properties.description: Retrieves entities that are connected to the specified entity through the given `property`.
properties.name: properties

relation_type.description: Filters results to show only connections linked by the selected CIDOC CRM property code. If 'geometry', 'types', 'depictions', or 'links' are specified in the 'show' parameter, these properties will also be displayed.
relation_type.name: relation_type

remove_empty_values.description: This will remove any keys which are empty or have no values assigned, e.g. []. This can reduce the load time.
remove_empty_values.name: remove_empty_values

search.description:
Search query for specific results.

 **Filterable categories**

 entityName, entityDescription, entityAliases, entityCidocClass, entitySystemClass, entityID, typeID, valueTypeID, typeIDWithSubs, typeName, beginFrom, beginTo, endFrom, endTo, relationToID

 **Values** 

 Values has to be a list of items. The items can be either a string, an integer or a tuple (see Notes). Strings need to be marked with “” or ‘’, while integers does not allow this. 
 
 *Notes*: 
 The category valueTypeID can search for values of a type ID. But it takes one or more two valued Tuple as list entry: (x,y). x is the type id and y is the searched value. This can be an int or a float, e.g: `{"operator":"lesserThan","values":[(3142,543.3)],"logicalOperator":"and"}`
 The date categories (beginFrom, beginTo, endFrom, endTo) only allow *one* value in the **values** field and it has to be formated the following way: YYYY-MM-DD. Month and day values need to filled up with 0, e.g. 800-01-01 

 **Compare operators** 

 equal, notEqual, like (1), greaterThan (2), greaterThanEqual (2), lesserThan (2)

The compare operators work like the mathematical operators. equal x=y, notEqual x!=y, greaterThan x>y , greaterThanEqual x>=y, lesserThan x<y, lesserThanEqual x<=y. The like operator searches for occurrence of the string, so a match can also occur in the middle of a word.

(1)Only for string based categories

(2)Only for beginFrom, beginTo, endFrom, endTo, valueTypeID

**Logical operators**

Not mandatory, OR is the default value. Logical operators handles, if the values are treated as OR or AND. 
 
 The following table outlines the supported operations for each field:

::

    |                  | equal
         | notEqual  | like      | greaterThan | greaterThanEqual | lesserThan | lesserThanEqual |
    |------------------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------------|------------------|------------|-----------------|
    | entityName       |     x      |     x      |    x       |             |                  |            |                 |
    | entityDescription|      x     |      x     |      x     |             |                  |            |                 |
    | entityAliases    |      x     |      x     |     x      |             |                  |            |                 |
    | entityCidocClass |      x     |      x     |      x     |             |                  |            |                 |
    | entitySystemClass|      x     |      x     |     x      |             |                  |            |                 |
    | typeName         |       x    |      x     |     x      |             |                  |            |                 |
    | entityID         |       x    |      x     |           |             |                  |            |                 |
    | typeID           |      x     |       x    |           |             |                  |            |                 |
    | valueTypeID      |      x     |      x     |           |      x       |    x              |    x        |      x           |
    | typeIDWithSubs   |      x     |       x    |           |             |                  |            |                 |
    | relationToID     |      x     |      x     |           |             |                  |            |                 |
    | beginFrom        |      x     |      x     |           |      x       |        x          |     x       |      x           |
    | beginTo          |      x     |      x     |           |      x       |          x        |      x      |       x          |
    | endFrom          |      x     |      x     |           |       x      |         x         |       x     |       x          |
    | endTo            |      x     |      x     |           |       x      |       x           |       x     |      x           |
search.name: search

show.description: Select which keys should not be displayed. This can improve performance
show.name: show

sort.description: Sorting result ascending or descending of the given column. Default value is asc.
sort.name: sort

system_classes.description: Retrieve entities based on the specified system classes
system_classes.name: system_classes

table_columns.description: Choose the columns you want to show. If nothing is entered, the most suitable template will be chosen. The order matters, so the first parameter will also be the first result in the return array.
table_columns.name: table_columns

type_id.description: Filter results to include only entities with the specified type ID or those linked to it.
type_id.name: type_id

url.description: A valid URL to use as input (e.g., https://openatlas.eu/). When used with an IIIF endpoint, this value replaces the base URL in all annotations.
url.name: url

view_classes.description: Retrieve entities based on the specified view classes.
view_classes.name: view_classes

message.example: The requested resource is not available due to a conflict.

Feature.description: A GeoJSON Feature object, linking a geometry to properties.

FeatureCollection.description: A collection of GeoJSON Feature objects.

locationId.description: A specific identifier for the location.

shapeType.description: A custom identifier for the shape's role or type.

message.example: You do not have access to the API. Please ask the data provider for permission.

Geometries.description:
Represents geographic data associated with an entity.
Can be explicitly null, a single GeoJSON geometry (Point, Polygon, or LineString),
or a GeoJSON FeatureCollection containing Features with specific properties.

LicensedFileOverviewModel.description: Dictionary keyed by numerical file IDs

LinearRing.description:
An array of four or more positions where the first and last positions are equivalent (they represent closed loops).
Used for defining polygon boundaries.

message.example: The requested resource does not exist.

PolygonCoordinates.description:
An array of LinearRing coordinate arrays. The first element MUST be the exterior ring.
Subsequent elements represent interior rings (holes).

Position.description:
A single position represented as an array of numbers.
The first two elements MUST be longitude and latitude (in that order).
An optional third element represents altitude. More elements are discouraged.
Example: [10.9787, 49.7606] or [10.9787, 49.7606, 150.5]

PositionArray.description: An array of two or more positions. Used for LineStrings.

begin.description: The start date of the entity

end.description: The end date of the entity

id.description: The unique identifier URL of the entity

system_class.description: The system class of the entity

SubunitsModel.description: A map of entity IDs (e.g., '50505') to an array of their subunit objects.

info.description: An API that allows user to access data from an OpenAtlas instance.
info.title: OpenAtlas API

get.description: Retrieves a list of backend configuration settings.
Get the chain of successive events also with forks but no super or sub events if they are not in the chain.
Retrieves entities based on their CIDOC CRM class code.
Retrieves all OpenAtlas classes with their label, CIDOC CRM mapping, view, icon and standard type ID. Label language is English by default but can be changed by `locale` parameter
Retrieves all OpenAtlas classes with their English label, CIDOC CRM mapping, view, icon and standard type ID.
Retrieves the image connected to the requested ID or entityID, if it has a licence.
Retrieves a list of object with id, system_class, label and related ids connected to one entity.
Retrieves entities linked to the entity with the provided `{entityId}`.
Retrieves information about a single entity.
Retrieves an Entity with its linked data for presentation sites
Downloads all information as CSV, XML, or JSON.
Retrieves a json with the entity IDs as keys and a list of file information for all images.
Retrieves a detailed GeoJSON of all chosen geometries, e.g. used for Leaflet maps.
Retrieves the manifest of the IIIF image.
Retrieves the last entered entities. `{limit}` represents the amount of entities (between 1 and 100). The pagination information is always null.
Retrieves all existing files with a licence and their display URL, thumbnail URL, extension and license. With the parameter `{file_id}` specific files can be retrieved.
Retrieves all entities linked with the provided property to the entity with the provided `{entityId}` in a recursive manner.
Retrieves a list of object with id, system_class, label and related ids
Retrieves OpenApi3 schema for this instance. Formats are `json` or `yaml`.

parameters.description: Choose the format for the results.
parameters.name: format

get.description: Retrieves all OpenAtlas CIDOC properties with their label, name, name inverse, code and translations.
Retrieves a list with entity ID, CIDOC CRM code, system class, or view class. You can combine these endpoints in a single query.

Searches the database for entries matching a provided search term. 
  Results are ordered with exact prefix matches appearing first, followed by entries containing the search term within their name or appellation.
Retrieves all subunits of a place in a special format, used by e.g. ARIADNEplus and THANADOS.

200.description: Successful response returning subunits for one or more entities.

get.description: Retrieves entities based on their OpenAtlas class name.
Retrieves system classes with a count of their instances.
Retrieves standard types in a hierarchical order.
Retrieves entities using provided `{entityId}` as their type. To get available types, Types Endpoints can be used.
Retrieves entities based on their OpenAtlas type ID including all connected subtypes. To get available types see Types Endpoints.
Retrieves all OpenAtlas types sorted by custom, places, standard, system and value types.
Retrieves a flat list of all types.
Retrieves entities based on their OpenAtlas view class.

tags.description: Information about a single entity. The requested information is provided in Linked Places format (LPF). Alternatively, Linked Open Art (LOUD), GeoJSON or RDFs, derived from the LOUD format, can be accessed.
tags.name: Entity Endpoint

tags.description: Combines several endpoints in one query.
tags.name: Entity Query Endpoint

tags.description: Information about multiple entities. The requested information is provided in Linked Places format (LPF). Alternatively, Linked Open Art (LOUD), GeoJSON or RDFs, derived from the LOUD format, can be accessed.
tags.name: Entities Endpoint

tags.description: Information about types. The requested information is provided in a custom JSON format.
tags.name: Type Endpoints
